tier_intro: This is where AI becomes a real productivity tool, not just a search engine. How you work, when you're sharp, what kills your flow, and how you actually get things done. No judgment — everyone's different. 5-7 minutes.

tier_complete.headline: Work profile locked.
tier_complete.body: AI now knows your energy rhythms, task patterns, and what actually gets you moving. Task management, scheduling, and project help just got a lot smarter. Next up: the deep personality layer — the most interesting tier.

questions.question: Your most productive day last week — describe it in one sentence. Which of these comes closest?

options.label: Locked in for 4 hours, crushed a huge thing, then was done for the day
Consistent pace all day — no huge highs, just solid output
On for 90 minutes, break, repeat — rhythm of focused bursts
Mostly reactive — handled what came up and did it well

questions.meta_profiling: Energy archetype is a high-level signal that shapes task scheduling, session length recommendations, and workload advice across all AI interactions.
questions.why_this_matters: AI schedules recommendations and task pacing to match your natural weekly rhythm.

questions.question: When during the day are you genuinely at your best — sharpest thinking, highest output?

options.label: Early morning (6-9am) — brain is clearest before the world wakes up
Mid-morning (9am-12pm)
Afternoon (1pm-5pm)
Evening (5pm-10pm) — I come alive later
Late night — when everything is quiet
Varies by day — no consistent peak

questions.why_this_matters: AI suggests timing for tasks and knows when you're likely to be sharp vs winding down.

questions.question: Before starting a big project, you...

options.label: Break it into every task I can think of before I touch anything
Sketch rough phases — enough to start, I'll detail as I go
Identify the first concrete step — planning too far ahead feels wasteful
Just start — the plan reveals itself once you're inside it

questions.meta_profiling: Task granularity determines how AI should present project breakdowns — full Gantt-style decomposition vs two-step next actions.
questions.why_this_matters: AI generates task breakdowns at the depth you actually use, not an overwhelming 50-item list.

questions.question: Your todo list typically covers...

options.label: Today — tomorrow will figure itself out
This week — I have a rough plan for the next 5 days
This month — I work in monthly sprints
Next quarter — I think in 90-day cycles
Year or more — I have a roadmap in my head

questions.why_this_matters: AI calibrates goal-setting and project suggestions to how far ahead you naturally think.

questions.question: You have a deadline in 3 days and you haven't started. You're most likely to...

options.label: Start today — this is normal for me. Deadline pressure is my fuel.
Break it into daily milestones and start immediately
Feel the pressure but have trouble starting — then cram at the end
I'd already be halfway done — I can't leave things to the last minute

questions.meta_profiling: Deadline behavior is one of the most predictive work dimensions. Stress_paralysis pattern deserves a follow-up for initiation strategies.
questions.why_this_matters: AI knows whether to front-load urgency or help you build structure to avoid last-minute crunches.

questions.question: When you're running on deadline pressure — quality outcome?

options.label: Often my best work — adrenaline sharpens me
Good enough, but I know I could do better with more time
Usually wish I'd started earlier — quality takes a hit

questions.question: What helps you break out of that starting paralysis?

options.label: Having someone else around — body doubling works for me
Breaking it into one tiny first step I can't say no to
Changing location — different space, different brain
Telling someone I'll have it done by X

questions.meta_profiling: body_doubling is a strong ADHD-associated coping strategy. Note for Tier 5 flag.

questions.question: Your ideal work setup — choose the one that sounds best for actually getting things done:

options.label: Solo, deep focus — no interruptions, full ownership
Async team — I like knowing others are involved, but on my schedule
Tight real-time pairing — working side by side with one person
Group energy — I'm better in a room with people working around me

questions.why_this_matters: AI adapts recommendations for solo workflows vs team coordination needs.

questions.question: New productivity app — everyone's raving about it, Notion/Linear/Obsidian-level hype. Your instinct?

options.label: Try it immediately — I'm always looking for a better setup
Read a few reviews, check if it actually solves a problem I have
Skeptical — I've been burned by tool-switching overhead too many times
Wait 6 months — if it's still around and people still love it, then I'll look

questions.meta_profiling: Tool philosophy affects how AI recommends new tools and systems. Early adopter = mention promising tools. Conservative = only suggest when current tool clearly failing.
questions.why_this_matters: AI knows whether to suggest new tools or help you get more out of what you already use.

questions.question: After finishing a project, your notes and documentation look like...

options.label: Thorough — I write things up properly so future-me doesn't suffer
Key points and lessons — enough to remember what mattered
Nothing — the work is done, I move on
Only if it was complex enough to warrant it

questions.meta_profiling: Documentation habit affects whether AI should produce written outputs proactively (for low-doc users) or keep things in flowing conversation (for high-doc users who capture themselves).
questions.why_this_matters: AI knows whether to proactively produce summaries and notes or trust you to capture what matters.

questions.question: You're deep in work, someone interrupts. You:

options.label: It takes me 20+ minutes to recover — interruptions are expensive for me
Annoying but I get back within 5 minutes
Handle it, write a quick note of where I was, resume
Sometimes welcome — a forced break can actually reset me

questions.meta_profiling: Very high context-switching cost combined with hard-to-start patterns = executive function compound signal. Note for Tier 5 ADHD flag.
questions.why_this_matters: AI knows whether to batch things before surfacing them or treat interruptions as acceptable.

questions.question: You do the same 3-click thing every single day. AI could automate it in 10 minutes. You...

options.label: Automate it immediately — every repeated manual task is debt
Depends how complex the setup is vs time saved
Keep it manual — automations break and then I'm stuck
Intend to automate it but somehow never get around to it

questions.meta_profiling: Automation tendency reveals relationship with technical friction and long-term thinking. High automators value AI as infrastructure, not just assistant.
questions.why_this_matters: Determines whether AI should proactively suggest automation opportunities or focus on manual execution.

questions.question: Plot your energy through a typical day. Which shape fits best?

options.label: High in the morning, drops after lunch, recovers slightly evening
Slow start, builds through the day, peaks afternoon/evening
Relatively flat — consistent energy throughout
Unpredictable spikes — I can't forecast when I'll be sharp

questions.meta_profiling: Energy curve combined with peak_hours gives full daily profile. Variable spikes + unpredictable = adaptive scheduling needed rather than fixed blocks.
questions.why_this_matters: AI matches task type to energy level — deep thinking work when sharp, admin when not.

questions.question: 2 hours into focused work. You need a break. Your ideal break looks like...

options.label: Complete mental switch — walk, gym, anything non-screen
Low-intensity screen — YouTube, social, something passive
5 minutes max — just enough to reset, then back
I rarely take proper breaks — I either keep going or crash

questions.meta_profiling: All-or-nothing break pattern + burst-rest archetype = unsustainable cycle warning. Note without flagging to user.
questions.why_this_matters: AI suggests break types that actually restore you vs ones that extend procrastination.

questions.question: How do you actually keep track of what needs doing? (Be honest — your real system, not the ideal one)

options.label: Dedicated task manager — Notion, Todoist, Linear, etc.
Notes app or document — Obsidian, Apple Notes, a running doc
Calendar — if it's not scheduled it doesn't exist
Mental list — it's all in my head and it mostly works
Nothing consistent — I'm looking for a better system

questions.meta_profiling: Mental-only task management + high task load = spill risk. Note for proactive reminders offer. Calendar-based = AI should suggest time-blocking.
questions.why_this_matters: AI works with your existing system instead of trying to impose a new one.

questions.question: Your ideal way to align with someone on something — a decision, a plan, a piece of work?

options.label: Async — shared doc, comments, no meeting needed
Short call or chat — 15 minutes sync beats 3 days of back-and-forth
Proper conversation — I think better in dialogue
Depends on complexity — quick = async, strategic = sync

questions.why_this_matters: AI suggests the right communication mode for different types of collaboration.

questions.question: You submit work. When do you want feedback?

options.label: Immediately — I'm still in context, fast feedback is most useful
Few hours — give it time to breathe, then review
Whenever the reviewer is ready — I'd rather have good feedback than fast
In rounds — quick initial reaction, then detailed when it's more developed

questions.meta_profiling: Feedback loop preference affects how AI delivers reviews. Immediate = AI should always respond to shared work right away. Quality-over-speed = AI can take time to compose thorough feedback.
questions.why_this_matters: AI times feedback delivery to match when you're most receptive and still engaged.

questions.question: The project is 90% done. You...

options.label: Ship the 90% — done and imperfect beats perfect and delayed
Do the last 10% properly — 90% is not done
Lose momentum — the exciting parts are done, the last 10% drags
Keep finding things to improve — 90% becomes 95% becomes 'still not ready'

questions.meta_profiling: novelty_seeker + perfectionist completion patterns are both common ADHD-adjacent patterns. Note compound with t4_q10 perfectionism and t2_q13 attention patterns.
questions.why_this_matters: AI knows how to help you actually ship — not just start.

questions.question: What's the best external lever to get you through that last 10%?

options.label: Hard deadline — make it real and I'll do it
Someone waiting for it — accountability to another person
Breaking the 10% into tiny steps — make the finish line visible

questions.question: How do you know when something is 'done enough' to ship?

options.label: Hard deadline forces my hand
Someone else tells me it's good enough
I have a mental checklist — if it passes, it ships
Honestly still figuring this out

questions.question: Your ideal work environment. What matters most?

options.label: Quiet — low noise or silence
Music or ambient sound — specific type matters
Natural light — windows, outdoors, daylight
Physical privacy — I can't think when people walk past me
Visible progress — checklist, board, something that shows I'm moving
Flexibility — I rotate between locations, one spot kills me

questions.meta_profiling: Workspace preferences are surprisingly stable personality indicators. Privacy + silence = introvert peak performance. Ambient audio + flexibility = stimulation-seeking.
questions.why_this_matters: AI can recommend and describe optimal environments when you're planning focus sessions.

questions.question: It's 8pm, you get a work message that could wait but you'd know about it. You...

options.label: Respond immediately — it'll take 2 minutes and then it's done
Read it, decide if it's urgent, respond only if it is
See it tomorrow — evenings are my time
Depends where I am in a project — crunch mode = always on, normal = off

questions.meta_profiling: Work-life boundary is both a preference and a health signal. No boundary + high work intensity = burnout risk indicator. Note for long-term pacing recommendations.
questions.why_this_matters: AI respects your boundaries around personal time — or helps you build them if you want that.

questions.question: When AI helps you with a task, which mode feels most natural?

options.label: Tell me the steps and I'll execute — just give me the playbook
Work through it together — I want to be involved in the thinking
Draft a complete output and I'll review and edit
Give me options and I'll pick and refine

questions.meta_profiling: Strongest single indicator for AI collaboration style. ai_first_human_reviews = high delegation comfort. collaborative = AI as thinking partner not tool.
questions.why_this_matters: Sets the fundamental dynamic of how you and AI divide labor.

questions.question: You've been sitting on a task for a week without starting. The real reason is usually...

options.label: Not clear enough — I don't know exactly what 'done' looks like
It feels too big — I don't know where to start
It's boring and I keep finding more interesting things
Fear of doing it wrong — I put it off because I might fail
Waiting on something or someone else — not fully in my control

questions.meta_profiling: Procrastination driver is the most actionable dimension for AI to address. Overwhelm = smaller steps. Ambiguity = clearer definition. Boredom = gamification. Performance anxiety = lower stakes framing.
questions.why_this_matters: AI addresses the real reason tasks stall — not just the symptom.

questions.question: When you're doing your best work, what's the single most important ingredient?
questions.placeholder: Describe what 'perfect working conditions' looks like for you (2-3 sentences max)
questions.meta_profiling: Open text response style here is itself a signal. Very short = low verbosity. Detailed + organized = structured thinker. Stream-of-consciousness = verbal processor.
questions.why_this_matters: Your words, not a dropdown. This goes directly into your AI profile as-is.
